course_description:
Analysis of scientific data and experiments: Design of experiments and ethical research. Data modelling and management. Exploratory data analysis. Randomness and probability. Statistical analysis including linear regression, analysis of variance, logistic regression, categorical data analysis, and non-parametric methods.

The aim of STAT1201 is to provide an understanding of the nature of scientific data and the subsequent need for statistical analysis. You will develop your statistical expertise and critical judgement in scientific studies, including an awareness of ethical issues in research and analysis. You will learn about the different types of data and how each can be visualised and summarised, and how you can make conclusions and predictions from the statistical analysis. You will also see that these statistical tools are based on simple mathematical ideas and associated assumptions.

UQ Public Timetable

Additional timetable information
Each week there are one or two lectures and practicals. Consult your personal timetable for the dates and times.
Lectures will held on campus and streamed with Zoom. See Blackboard for the Zoom link. Lectures will also be recorded.
The Practicals are held on-campus for in-person students and via Zoom for external students. Practicals involve collaborative group work and so will
not
be recorded.ﾠ
course_aims: The aim of STAT1201 is to provide an understanding of the nature of scientific data and the subsequent need for statistical analysis. You will develop your statistical expertise and critical judgement in scientific studies, including an awareness of ethical issues in research and analysis. You will learn about the different types of data and how each can be visualized and summarized, and how you can make conclusions and predictions from the statistical analysis. You will also see that these statistical tools are based on simple mathematical ideas and associated assumptions.

learning_outcomes.description: Explain the nature of scientific data and the need for statistical analysis.
Identify factors related to the design of a scientific study, including sample size and power.
Identify and critically evaluate the role of data analysis and statistics in scientific research and publications.
Identify and critically evaluate ethical issues in scientific research.
Demonstrate a foundational knowledge of statistical methods by being able to carry out simple statistical procedures by hand.
Use statistical software appropriately and confidently for exploratory data analysis and to make relevant statistical conclusions.
Plan and carry out a research project, and analyse and communicate the results.

assessment_summary.category: Participation/ Student contribution, Quiz, Tutorial/ Problem Set
assessment_summary.title: Online Quizzes
assessment_summary.due_date: Individual questions are due by 2:00pm on the Monday after the quiz opens. Collaborative questions are due by the end of your allocated practical.

assessment_details.title: Online Quizzes
assessment_details.category: Participation/ Student contribution, Quiz, Tutorial/ Problem Set
assessment_details.due_date: Individual questions are due by 2:00pm on the Monday after the quiz opens. Collaborative questions are due by the end of your allocated practical.
assessment_details.task_description: In each teaching week, there will be one or two quizzes to complete. Each quiz will involve 10 questions: Five questions are individual questions . These questions will open at 2:00pm on Monday and close at 2:00pm on the following Monday (except as noted below). Most will involve analysing data using RStudio and completing these will be an important opportunity to learn the details of working with RStudio. You are encouraged to discuss these questions using the Ed Discussion Board . Five questions are collaborative questions . These questions will be completed during your allocated practical. Your best eight quiz marks will each count 2% towards your overall grade (for a maximum of 16%). Please note that the following schedule provides the opening week of each quiz. Week 1 - Quiz 1 Week 2 - Quiz 2 and Quiz 3 Week 3 - Quiz 4 and Quiz 5 Week 4 - Quiz 6 Week 6 - Quiz 7 and Quiz 8 Week 7 - Quiz 9 Week 8 - Quiz 10 For example, the individual questions on Quiz 2 and Quiz 3 will both open at 2:00pm on Monday, December 2nd (Week 2), and will close at 2:00pm on Monday, December 9th. The collaborative questions for Quiz 2 will be completed during Practical 2 (Tuesday, December 3rd) while the collaborative questions for Quiz 3 will be completed during Practical 3 (Friday, December 6th). Due to holidays, there will be two exceptions: Quiz 6 individual questions will be due at 5:00pm on Friday, December 20th and Quiz 10 individual questions will be due at 5:00pm on Friday, January 24th . Unless otherwise specified, this assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT). Students are advised that the use of AI or MT technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Complete via Blackboard
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
An approved extension will be applied to the individual questions and will be taken as an exemption from the collaborative questions in the practical.
Late submission
If you fail to meet the submission deadline for the individual questions you will not receive any marks unless an extension has been approved.

assessment_details.title: Paper Review
assessment_details.task_description: The Paper Review involves finding a scientific paper of interest to you through the library. You can choose any paper you like as long as it has a P-value and a DOI, and was published no earlier than 2021. Each paper can only be reviewed by one group so once you have your paper you must register it on Blackboard. You will provide a brief summary of the use of statistical inference in the paper you choose and an essay discussing the ethical issues involved in the study. See Blackboard for example reviews. The review is submitted using a form on Blackboard. The combined word length of the statistical component should not exceed 250 words, while the ethics essay has a maximum of 500 words. The Paper Review may be completed in groups of up to three students. Use the tools on Blackboard if you want to form a group. Groups must be formed by the due date. This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT). Students are advised that the use of AI or MT technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Complete via Blackboard
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
See ADDITIONAL ASSESSMENT INFORMATION for the extension information relating to this assessment item.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
The late submission penalty will be deducted per day for up to 7 calendar days, at which point any submission will not receive any marks unless an extension has been approved. Each 24 hour block is recorded from the time the submission is due.

assessment_details.title: Research Project
assessment_details.task_description: For the Research Project you will design a study, carry it out with a population of virtual subjects to collect your data, and then analyse the results and present them in the form of a presentation, as would be given at a scientific conference. Your presentation will be in the form of a video of roughly 6 minutes in length and featuring each person in the group talking to camera (separately or together). Further information about creating your video is provided on Blackboard. The research project may be completed in groups of up to three students. Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) may be used to support students in brainstorming their research topic and developing their study design, addressing the first two criteria of this assessment task. The remaining criteria evaluate students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT). Students are advised that the use of AI or MT technologies to develop responses to the remainder of the criteria is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. assessment_details.title: Final Examination
assessment_details.task_description: The Final Examination in this course will be held during the end-of-semester examination period. It will be an held on campus for in-person students and invigilated over Zoom for external students. The Final Examination will cover the statistical content of the course, as covered in UQ Extend and the lectures and practicals. You will be provided with a sheet of useful definitions and formulas. This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT). Students are advised that the use of AI or MT technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
